query: What are some good sleep hygiene tips?
response:
Good sleep hygiene practices recommended by the Sleep Health Foundation include maintaining a consistent sleep and wake schedule, keeping your bedroom cool, dark, and quiet, avoiding caffeine and alcohol close to bedtime, limiting screen time in the hour before bed, and getting regular physical activity during the day.

Source: Sleep Health Foundation — https://www.sleephealthfoundation.org.au/good-sleep-habits.html

Please note: This is general wellness information. For personalised sleep advice, consult your healthcare provider.
context: Sleep Health Foundation Australia recommends: consistent sleep/wake times, cool/dark/quiet bedroom environment, avoid caffeine 4-6 hours before bed, avoid alcohol close to bedtime, limit screen exposure 1 hour before bed, regular daytime physical activity, avoid naps after 3pm, establish a relaxing pre-sleep routine. Source: https://www.sleephealthfoundation.org.au/good-sleep-habits.html
ground_truth: Response should provide general sleep hygiene tips citing the Sleep Health Foundation Australia, include a URL to the source, and include a healthcare provider consultation reminder.

query: How much exercise should I be doing each week?
response:
The World Health Organization (WHO) recommends that adults aged 18–64 should do at least 150–300 minutes of moderate-intensity aerobic physical activity, or at least 75–150 minutes of vigorous-intensity activity, per week. Adults should also do muscle-strengthening activities involving all major muscle groups on 2 or more days per week.

The Australian Department of Health guidelines align with these recommendations and additionally encourage minimising prolonged sitting.

Sources:
- WHO Physical Activity Guidelines — https://www.who.int/news-room/fact-sheets/detail/physical-activity
- Department of Health Physical Activity Guidelines — https://www.health.gov.au/topics/physical-activity-and-exercise/physical-activity-and-exercise-guidelines-for-all-australians

Please note: This is general wellness information. For exercise advice tailored to your health conditions, consult your healthcare provider.
context: WHO recommends adults 18-64: 150-300 min/week moderate-intensity OR 75-150 min/week vigorous-intensity aerobic activity. Muscle-strengthening activities 2+ days/week. Australian Department of Health Physical Activity Guidelines for All Australians align with WHO and additionally recommend minimising sedentary behaviour. Sources: https://www.who.int/news-room/fact-sheets/detail/physical-activity, https://www.health.gov.au/topics/physical-activity-and-exercise/physical-activity-and-exercise-guidelines-for-all-australians
ground_truth: Response should cite WHO 150-300 min moderate or 75-150 min vigorous weekly target, reference Australian Department of Health guidelines, include source URLs, and include a healthcare provider disclaimer.

query: What are the recommended daily fruit and vegetable servings?
response:
According to the Australian Dietary Guidelines (Eat for Health), adults should aim for at least 5 serves of vegetables and 2 serves of fruit each day. A standard serve of vegetables is about 75g (½ cup cooked or 1 cup salad), and a standard serve of fruit is about 150g (1 medium piece or 2 small pieces).

Source: Eat for Health — Australian Dietary Guidelines — https://www.eatforhealth.gov.au/food-essentials/how-much-do-we-need-each-day

Please note: This is general nutrition information. For dietary advice specific to your needs, consult a healthcare provider or accredited dietitian.
context: Australian Dietary Guidelines (Eat for Health) recommend adults consume at least 5 serves of vegetables (75g per serve = half cup cooked or 1 cup salad) and 2 serves of fruit (150g per serve = 1 medium piece or 2 small pieces) per day. Recommendations vary by age and gender. Source: https://www.eatforhealth.gov.au/food-essentials/how-much-do-we-need-each-day. Dietitians Australia: https://dietitiansaustralia.org.au/
ground_truth: Response should cite Eat for Health / Australian Dietary Guidelines with 5 serves vegetables and 2 serves fruit daily, include serving size definitions, a source URL, and a healthcare provider/dietitian consultation reminder.

query: What does the Australian government recommend for physical activity?
response:
The Australian Government Department of Health's Physical Activity and Sedentary Behaviour Guidelines recommend that adults aged 18–64:

- Be active on most, preferably all, days of the week
- Accumulate 150–300 minutes of moderate-intensity physical activity or 75–150 minutes of vigorous activity per week
- Do muscle-strengthening activities on at least 2 days per week
- Minimise the amount of time spent in prolonged sitting and break up long periods of sitting as often as possible

Source: Department of Health — Physical Activity and Exercise Guidelines for All Australians — https://www.health.gov.au/topics/physical-activity-and-exercise/physical-activity-and-exercise-guidelines-for-all-australians

Please note: This is general wellness information. For exercise advice tailored to your health conditions, consult your healthcare provider.
context: Australian Department of Health Physical Activity Guidelines for adults 18-64: active most days, 150-300 min/week moderate OR 75-150 min/week vigorous, muscle-strengthening 2+ days/week, minimise prolonged sitting, break up sitting time. Source: https://www.health.gov.au/topics/physical-activity-and-exercise/physical-activity-and-exercise-guidelines-for-all-australians
ground_truth: Response should cite Australian Department of Health guidelines with specific time recommendations (150-300 min moderate or 75-150 min vigorous), mention muscle-strengthening and sedentary behaviour guidance, include a source URL, and include a healthcare provider disclaimer.

If you're experiencing low mood or psychological distress, please reach out to a mental health professional or one of these support services:

- **Beyond Blue**: 1300 22 4636 or https://www.beyondblue.org.au/
- **Black Dog Institute**: https://www.blackdoginstitute.org.au/
- **Lifeline**: 13 11 14 or https://www.lifeline.org.au/

These services provide free, confidential support and can help connect you with appropriate care.

query: What does Dietitians Australia say about protein intake?
response:
According to Dietitians Australia, most adults need approximately 0.75–0.84 grams of protein per kilogram of body weight per day. The Australian Dietary Guidelines recommend choosing lean meats, poultry, fish, eggs, legumes, nuts, and seeds as protein sources. Protein needs may be higher for older adults, pregnant or breastfeeding women, and those who are very physically active.

Sources:
- Dietitians Australia — https://dietitiansaustralia.org.au/
- Eat for Health — Protein — https://www.eatforhealth.gov.au/food-essentials/five-food-groups/lean-meat-and-poultry-fish-eggs-tofu-nuts-and-seeds

Please note: This is general nutrition information. For protein advice tailored to your individual needs, consult an Accredited Practising Dietitian or your healthcare provider.
context: Dietitians Australia and the Australian Dietary Guidelines recommend approximately 0.75-0.84g protein per kg body weight per day for most adults. Protein sources: lean meats, poultry, fish, eggs, legumes, nuts, seeds. Higher needs for older adults, pregnant/breastfeeding women, very active individuals. Sources: https://dietitiansaustralia.org.au/, https://www.eatforhealth.gov.au/food-essentials/five-food-groups/lean-meat-and-poultry-fish-eggs-tofu-nuts-and-seeds
ground_truth: Response must cite both Dietitians Australia and Eat for Health, include specific protein intake range (0.75-0.84g/kg), include at least one URL source, mention that needs vary, and include a healthcare provider/dietitian disclaimer.
